recentTweets.text: Israel says it struck Palestinian Islamic Jihad headquarters in Damascus today. We found what neighbors said was an apartment vacated years ago and next door a badly wounded elderly woman and an injured grey parrot. https://t.co/DkBJQyMTgy

This explains why one woman fleeing Rafah destroyed her family photos... "I burned my personal pictures in case someone comes into my house and does something bad to them. It was a very difficult feeling to burn your memories" she tells NPR https://t.co/8OxZTz0kYC
The analysts, the journalists, the doctors, the grandmothers and grandfathers, the women and children - an entire civilization is being erased.

Ukrainian girl playing on top of a tank at the WWII museum in Kyiv. Her mom says they came on this day celebrating the end of that war to commemorate those who died so others could live. https://t.co/6H93QMMI5L
Ukrainian musicians playing folk music on banduras in Easter weekend in Lviv. https://t.co/mAZKoM4Bel

A lovely woman and lovely moment in the flower market in Lviv, Ukraine. The pussy-willow bouquets are the Ukrainian version of palm fronds - blessed at churches on Palm Sunday. https://t.co/IFlrxhliOf
In Lviv, Ukraine street performer turns around the day of Ukrainian children, many of them displaced by war. Pure magic. https://t.co/Fdfz3sn0Df
In the wonderful market in Qamishli, the Rojava region in northeastern Syria, a shopkeeper who has been selling yogurt and thick water buffalo cream for half a century. https://t.co/XbsADAG3iu
Iraq's state TV battles racism with the historic hiring of a remarkable young woman: A Black Iraqi’s Sudden Career in TV News: ‘They Wanted to See All Colors’ https://t.co/kQSExpUrU2

Thousands turn out for burials in Qamishli of SDF fighters killed defending prison in northeast Syria from ISIS attack. 40 fighters and 70 prison guards and workers killed in the fighting. https://t.co/ccniw8rCKN

U.S.-led coalition says U.S. ground troops in armored fighting vehicles are now backing Kurdish-Syrian forces battling ISIS troops at Hasaka prison. SDF tightening cordon around the prison with hundreds of child detainees being held hostage.
Beautiful Baghdad... https://t.co/OuO2WUkqR5
Sometimes in Baghdad you see things that give you hope - cleaned up parks, playgrounds….and then there is this stuffed wolf. https://t.co/to8jCjpEDm

A little known piece of Iraqi history at the new Iraqi Media Museum: this mechanical singing bird was presented by Adolph Hitler to King Ghazi on the opening of Iraqi state radio in 1936. A recording of its song still opens state radio broadcasts. https://t.co/OeXWTdaHGy
The most beautiful greens at a sidewalk produce stand in Baghdad. https://t.co/Mgxgab2l7b

Iraqi Kurds at the Belarus border are describing a vision of hell. Beatings by Belarus police and if you make it to the Lithuania side, beatings and Tasers. And still people are so desperate for a better life than their country can offer they are willing to risk all that.
Kurdish still life. Halabja pomegranates. https://t.co/mNy2SBbKFu
Good morning. Fifteen seconds of swallows over Baghdad https://t.co/4Kqxh36KwN
Security forces voting in Baghdad. This officer brought his daughter to show her how elections work. Iraq’s fifth national elections since 2003 - pretty good for the region. https://t.co/gSg8O333V5
Women Enslaved by ISIS Say They Did Not Consent to a Film About Them https://t.co/twZMXCUDoR

An important story, with @sangarkhaleel, about who has the right over ISIS survivors' images, voices and stories:  Women Enslaved by ISIS Say They Did Not Consent to a Film About Them https://t.co/twZMXDceNr
Great she is there. Unfortunate the editor didn't notice that when she says they are chanting 'death to America' they are actually chanting is 'God is greater'.
In a rare summer rain shower in Iraqi Kurdistan, Yazidi kids play near a temple. Sheer joy. The ISIS genocide killed and enslaved thousands but didn't erase the community. https://t.co/GZxiodnTMZ

Amid the anniversary of the 2014 ISIS genocide this week, a small moment of joy. Yazidi children play in the wind and rain at a temple in the Iraqi Kurdistan region today. ISIS tried and failed to extinguish the community. https://t.co/Nn3WycDOn2

One of Iraq's most prominent human rights activists bids goodbye in Basra to her son who was kidnapped and shot dead. The government calls them unknown groups but everyone seems to know the groups carrying out these killings.

Peaceful student protest in Baghdad’s Tahrir square this evening calling for end to impunity for killers of activists. Iraqi military command issued orders barring photographers, video cameras. More security forces than protesters. https://t.co/uCufjUmRjk
My cat editor is not impressed… https://t.co/EtgkOnouUE

Drone attack on Baghdad airport Wednesday resulted in minor damage to State Department compound inside the airport complex, some treated for smoke inhalation. Today, U.S. offers up to $3 million reward for info on those targeting American diplomatic missions.

Mourning the tragic passing of Dr Nemam Ghafouri - founder of Joint Help for Kurdistan and an extraordinary human being who devoted the past five years of her life to helping #Yazidi women and children. She moved mountains. https://t.co/LdJiT07jyL

This may be the most pointed Twitter comment I've seen from a diplomat in #Iraq. The man below, who publicly accused an #Iran-backed militia of kidnapping his activist son two years ago, was shot in the head in the south of Iraq this week. Bravery is dangerous.
#Pope Francis arrives to rapturous welcome in Qaraqosh - biggest Christian town in #Iraq’s Ninevah plains and one celebrating its survival from ISIS. https://t.co/n6Bc5HQULc
#Pope Francis in Ur notes everyone has suffered from violence in #Iraq but specifically points out #Yazidis - the men killed, the women and children captured, those still missing.
A lovely piece.... Baghdad: forever on the brink https://t.co/yWvJuOksQk
Early spring weather in the beautiful #Kurdistan Region. https://t.co/fS1hyjcHQi

Nobel laureate Nadia Murad with the coffin of her brother - one of 103 #Yazidi victims of #ISIS genocide buried today in the #Iraqi village of Kocho after being identified by DNA testing. Likely thousands more in mass graves. https://t.co/9m0qArY4FI
In the #Yazidi village of Kocho, remains of 103 victims of #ISIS genocide returned to their families for burial today after being exhumed from a mass grave two years ago. https://t.co/wftLsLP1bH
#Baghdad! Not always what you think.

Striking that not a single person we talked to here believed it was #ISIS. They all believe #Iraqi political parties are jockeying for power and killing the poor in the process: Suicide Bombing in Crowded #Baghdad Market Kills at Least 32 https://t.co/v2DYuPifqY

Lovely to be in #Baghdad with ⁦@nytimes⁩. Where else can I have conversations like this? I asked Amar  Musa selling tinsel and Christmas trees in the street how business was and he recited one of his poems: ‘I am a cigarette and Iraq is the grave’. https://t.co/yQCLFBLV2z

A happier ending than most: #Mosul orphanage official tells #Yazidi mom in Canada today her daughter kidnapped six years ago by #ISIS has been identified. (video courtesy Sukaina Yunis) The girl's story here:  https://t.co/tv84bAZWIR https://t.co/EY3mBYBkE0

A happier ending to a terrible story. Sonya, the kidnapped daughter of a #Yazidi mom enslaved by #ISIS, walks out of a Mosul orphanage today after DNA tests confirm her identity. Her story here:  https://t.co/tv84bAZWIR (Photo courtesy Sukaina Yunis) https://t.co/OaEffEQiOY

My final @npr conversation with the wonderful @arishapiro @npract before joining @nytimes. Thank you to an enduringly great organization, cherished colleagues and editors and wonderful listeners. Photo Mosul 2017 w @SangarKhaleel  https://t.co/KFfSGqtkai https://t.co/ex2mUDHp1v

And the first visit by a pope to #Iraq. Saddam denied Vatican requests for a visit in fear of large gatherings he wasn't entirely in control of. Iraq is known as the land of prophets - including Ur - reputed birthplace of the Prophet Abraham.
Just a regular Thursday night traffic jam in #Baghdad... https://t.co/1IKivo3DFx
Thank you Diaa - I'm thrilled but sad to be leaving NPR - a truly wonderful place with amazing, talented and incredibly dedicated colleagues.

Hundreds of #Yazidi families moving home to #Sinjar after six years in displacement camps. Returning to damaged, looted houses and no electricity but as one returnee says ‘it’s better to have bread and water in your own home than meat and rice anywhere else’. https://t.co/T1gDjosoDH

#Jordan isn't really 'cautiously welcoming' the #UAE-Israel agreement with its polite statement, it's warning that the conflict will deepen unless this leads to an independent #Palestinian state - a prospect that seems further away with this deal.

Such sad news. #Iraq counter-terrorism expert and government advisor Hisham al-Hashimi shot dead tonight in Baghdad. There were few people who knew more about ISIS and were as generous with their knowledge.
Six years after #Yazidis lost their lives and their homes, why are children still living in tents in the mud? Anyone?
When this happens in the Middle East, people call them extremists...

UK court releases findings that #UAE's Sheikh Mohammed ordered the kidnapping of two of his daughters - one abducted after being sedated, the other subject to 'physical abuse amounting to torture'. Takes some of the shine off #Dubai. https://t.co/D1Yj2W6pvz
What did you do with the women? This looks like almost every official meeting in the Middle East.

I would like to cover an #Iraq where I don't have to write about 15-year-old boys sneaking out of the house to demonstrate for basic rights and being shot dead by security forces. Three of the latest protestors killed, including teenager Raymond Salem.

Wildly conflicting figures on how many but appeared to us to be to be at least a couple hundred thousand - some wire stories saying just 'thousands'. Extremely disciplined no matter the numbers. Sadrists and others sent their message and went home.

Twelve-year-old in Baghdad near the protest, left school after second grade. Sitting down because he's tired from  selling gum to support his brothers and sisters. Kids like him are part of the reason the protestors persist. #Iraqprotests https://t.co/riJB4axhmb
Air raid sirens coming from US embassy and explosion that sounded like rocket landing but unclear whether in #Baghdad green zone or nearby neighborhood.
Rocket attacks near Erbil apparently hit empty fields - inc unexploded rocket near airport. No attack on US consulate according to security officials.

Given the reports of bots behind this campaign, I think it's safe to say this is a real person, with a real message in Baghdad's real Tahrir square. A particularly brave one since so many young #Iraqis are being arrested or kidnapped for protesting.

Another #Baghdad rocket attack aiming at the US embassy that hit #Iraqi targets. Six civilians reported injured when rockets landed near entrance to green zone and in residential area not far from the embassy. Despite that #Iraqi forces continuing to protect the embassy area.
Explosion, presumably a rocket by the thud, in Baghdad green zone, followed by second explosion. Air raid sirens going off. No word yet on whether there were casualties.

Just a regular Thursday night traffic jam in #Baghdad... https://t.co/1IKivo3DFx

Hundreds of #Yazidi families moving home to #Sinjar after six years in displacement camps. Returning to damaged, looted houses and no electricity but as one returnee says ‘it’s better to have bread and water in your own home than meat and rice anywhere else’. https://t.co/T1gDjosoDH

Such sad news. #Iraq counter-terrorism expert and government advisor Hisham al-Hashimi shot dead tonight in Baghdad. There were few people who knew more about ISIS and were as generous with their knowledge.

I would like to cover an #Iraq where I don't have to write about 15-year-old boys sneaking out of the house to demonstrate for basic rights and being shot dead by security forces. Three of the latest protestors killed, including teenager Raymond Salem.

Twelve-year-old in Baghdad near the protest, left school after second grade. Sitting down because he's tired from  selling gum to support his brothers and sisters. Kids like him are part of the reason the protestors persist. #Iraqprotests https://t.co/riJB4axhmb
Air raid sirens coming from US embassy and explosion that sounded like rocket landing but unclear whether in #Baghdad green zone or nearby neighborhood.
Rocket attacks near Erbil apparently hit empty fields - inc unexploded rocket near airport. No attack on US consulate according to security officials.

Given the reports of bots behind this campaign, I think it's safe to say this is a real person, with a real message in Baghdad's real Tahrir square. A particularly brave one since so many young #Iraqis are being arrested or kidnapped for protesting.

Another #Baghdad rocket attack aiming at the US embassy that hit #Iraqi targets. Six civilians reported injured when rockets landed near entrance to green zone and in residential area not far from the embassy. Despite that #Iraqi forces continuing to protect the embassy area.
Explosion, presumably a rocket by the thud, in Baghdad green zone, followed by second explosion. Air raid sirens going off. No word yet on whether there were casualties.
@jeffersonmorley @ArianeTabatabai @realDonaldTrump The US-backed prime minister of Iraq was the source...Outgoing but still PM.

This is stunning - #Iraq prime minister tells parliament US troops should leave. Says @realDonaldTrump called him to ask him to mediate with #Iran and then ordered drone strike on Soleimani. Says Soleimani carrying response to Saudi initiative to defuse tension when he was hit.

After 2003 #Baghdad's airport road was one of the deadliest roads in #Iraq. It's been years since then - renovation included versions of iconic Iraqi paintings - now marked with shrapnel from the US drone strike. A lot of Iraqis fear being plunged back into the abyss. https://t.co/KwzRNUxp7o

One of bloodiest days in two months of #Iraq anti-government protests as security forces kill 27 people. Most of them in Nassriyah to keep protestors off a bridge. #Iran-backed militia now part of Iraqi security forces sends fighters to Najaf after #Iran embassy burned there.

Almost unbelievable - #Iranian consulate burned to the ground by #Iraqi protestors in Najaf. After setting it on fire, they blocked firefighters from putting it out. Diplomats believed evacuated weeks ago. This kind of anger won't be extinguished by wrangling over election law.

#Iraqi nurses in #Baghdad's Tahrir square. After their hospital shifts they come to treat the wounded near the front lines of the protest. 'My parents think I'm at work' one says. #IraqiProtests https://t.co/C8wjAEFeJz

At #Baghdad protest today when I could open my eyes again after a cloud of tear gas descended, a policeman with a bunch of plastic roses with tiny white teddy bears handed me one. And I think that pretty much explains why #journalism is the best job in the world.

In #Baghdad where my hotel has received security orders banning #journalists from staying in rooms facing river or on high floors where you can see Tahrir Square protest unfolding. Transported to Saddam’s time when entire hotel wings were blocked so we couldn’t see things.

Statement from #Iraq military command saying US troops don't have approval to stay despite US assertions they will be deployed from #Syria to Western Iraq. US Administration seems to have neglected to consult the Iraq government... US defense secretary to discuss with Iraq today.

Iraqi women posing on steps of presidential palace after working with President @BarhamSalih on landmark draft bill to criminalize domestic violence. Controversial in #Iraq, the bill would end protection for assault within families and allow safe houses for victims of violence. https://t.co/7mHWeiAK38

In #Sinjar today. Beautiful #Yazidi holiday celebration at the Sharfadin temple. A day after commemoration of the #ISIS massacre in the Yazidi village of #Kocho in northern #Iraq. Smaller and more vulnerable but the community lives on. Stories coming @NPR https://t.co/UdARcjxwcg

Grateful to witness this. Cocho village in #Sinjar today on the fifth anniversary of the #ISIS massacre. The indescribable sorrow of #Yazidi women whose families were ripped apart in killings and  kidnappings at the heart of the #genocide. Stories coming @NPR https://t.co/YNVU7sCqxW
From Cocho village in Sinjar today - #Yazidi women mourning the more than 400 dead in the #ISIS massacre five years ago. #Yazidi #Genocide https://t.co/rVbcBuvl9U

French #ISIS member, Leonardo Mark, 33, Paris postal worker says ISIS declared him medically unfit for fighting. Says he taught in ISIS school and twice tried to escape. Asks forgiveness from Iraqis and to be sent back to #France. Baghdad court sentences him to death by hanging.

Absolutely extraordinary move in context of the region - #Yazidi religious leaders say Yazidi women who had children after being raped by #ISIS fighters can keep their children. Hundreds of women forced to give up them up to go home. Question now of whether they can find them.

Beautiful #Yazidi new year celebration on the mountain at Lalesh tonight. Five years after the #ISIS genocide, flames lit to celebrate earth reborn, victory of light over darkness. https://t.co/1qcs2SL3B8

On my way out of #Rojava #Syria with two particularly lasting memories - told by ISIS wives at Camp Hol I'm an infidel who needs to cover my hair and young village men on a flooded road cheerfully standing in the water to show us where the road ends and get us safely across.

There's a boy lying here in #Mosul's old city last year. Not clear if he was from an Arab #ISIS family or a Yazidi captive but he was a boy, killed in the fighting. He's not one of the 800+ children recorded killed. He was buried in a mass grave. We should not forget him. https://t.co/VamOLG9luJ
Nice to hear #Kurdish flight announcement on #Iraqi Airways in addition to Arabic and English. Might seem like a small thing but hugely important.

An #Iraqi treasure is gone. Dr Lamia al-Gailani. An #archaeologist in #Iraq and London for the past half-century currently working on the new #Basra museum, passed away suddenly in Amman. Dr Gailani had an infectious love of archaeology and of life. She will be greatly missed. https://t.co/I8D9598VlV

A moment I didn’t think I’d see - #Iraqi musician serenades US Marine General Austin Renforth and Iraqi General Jalil Rubaie on very relaxed walk-about among the crowds in downtown #Baghdad https://t.co/N5tLjTIovJ
Amazing. A concert near the building in #Mosul where ISIS threw people to their deaths.

#Mosul night-life. A well-kept secret - after ISIS east Mosul comes alive at night. Restaurants on the river packed with people. Amusement parks and boat rides on the Tigris. Families who can't afford any of those picnic on the sidewalk. https://t.co/xZn5LlgWoC
In #Basra's Aleea neighborhood. As we walked through one of the poorest neighborhoods in the poorest big city in #Iraq, people kept inviting us for lunch. https://t.co/Ybu5PMIJyL

#Mosul Book Festival - crowds lining up for donated books. #ISIS banned non-religious books, music and almost everything else. Now there's a hunger for culture again. Thousands of people came out for books, music and theatre in a park where ISIS trained child fighters. https://t.co/e61DRevO74

The girls of #Mosul salute you. Handing out sweets and greeting visitors at today’s Book Festival under the title ‘I am Iraqi - I read’. Thousands of people enjoying books and music at a park where #ISIS trained child fighters. https://t.co/mCE5RVUO7x
Voting in #Baghdad - 84 year old voter and his wife, 75, in Athamiya neighborhood. They say they’re hopeful for the future.  #Iraq #elections https://t.co/aM9tGI4yUT

In #Iraq's post-ISIS #Mosul - a not exactly licensed but very cheerful new fast-food place. 'Iraq is a country of wonders - you can find anything here' says the owner when I express surprise to see the golden arches in Mosul. @npr #RoadTrip https://t.co/U5vfNmRzc1
#Kurdish leader Massoud Barzani tells @NPR in first interview since referendum no regrets on vote despite consequences. https://t.co/bqx4OOlEN2
First day of school in #Mosul - with #ISIS gone, public schools open for first time in three years. Few books though. https://t.co/odGl3lSqqz
The only non-Kurdish flag at rally going on now in #Erbil. Israel the sole country to support independence referendum https://t.co/MMGxTsOrLN
The only non-Kurdish flag at rally going on now in #Erbil. Israel the sole country to support independence referendum https://t.co/kGf2jKxhfi
#Iraqi man escaping # Mosul with his nightingale. Keeping birds banned under #ISIS. He says he hid it for three years. https://t.co/33afgOo4sF
Iraqi Yazidis - among the biggest ISIS victims - denied boarding on US-bound flights despite visas. Cleared Iraqi refugees also barred.
#Kurdish president @masoud_barzani talks Mosul, #Iran and prisoners held by IS in my PBS @NewsHourWorld interview http://t.co/bPk6q1HRkB

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 77 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Jane Arraf. The social-media posture reads as security-first / counterterrorism frame because the strongest matched frame is security and counterterrorism language. Top matched terms: Iraq / Baghdad / Kirkuk, Yazidi / Sinjar, Kurds / Kurdish, Syria / Rojava.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around security and counterterrorism language. I count 15 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 37 security-frame hits, 16 supportive or diplomatic hits, 14 humanitarian/minority hits, and 2 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2022-02-07T09:42:28Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.

tweets.text: Israel says it struck Palestinian Islamic Jihad headquarters in Damascus today. We found what neighbors said was an apartment vacated years ago and next door a badly wounded elderly woman and an injured grey parrot. https://t.co/DkBJQyMTgy

This explains why one woman fleeing Rafah destroyed her family photos... "I burned my personal pictures in case someone comes into my house and does something bad to them. It was a very difficult feeling to burn your memories" she tells NPR https://t.co/8OxZTz0kYC
The analysts, the journalists, the doctors, the grandmothers and grandfathers, the women and children - an entire civilization is being erased.

A little known piece of Iraqi history at the new Iraqi Media Museum: this mechanical singing bird was presented by Adolph Hitler to King Ghazi on the opening of Iraqi state radio in 1936. A recording of its song still opens state radio broadcasts. https://t.co/OeXWTdaHGy
The most beautiful greens at a sidewalk produce stand in Baghdad. https://t.co/Mgxgab2l7b

Iraqi Kurds at the Belarus border are describing a vision of hell. Beatings by Belarus police and if you make it to the Lithuania side, beatings and Tasers. And still people are so desperate for a better life than their country can offer they are willing to risk all that.
Kurdish still life. Halabja pomegranates. https://t.co/mNy2SBbKFu
Good morning. Fifteen seconds of swallows over Baghdad https://t.co/4Kqxh36KwN
Security forces voting in Baghdad. This officer brought his daughter to show her how elections work. Iraq’s fifth national elections since 2003 - pretty good for the region. https://t.co/gSg8O333V5
Women Enslaved by ISIS Say They Did Not Consent to a Film About Them https://t.co/twZMXCUDoR

An important story, with @sangarkhaleel, about who has the right over ISIS survivors' images, voices and stories:  Women Enslaved by ISIS Say They Did Not Consent to a Film About Them https://t.co/twZMXDceNr
Great she is there. Unfortunate the editor didn't notice that when she says they are chanting 'death to America' they are actually chanting is 'God is greater'.
In a rare summer rain shower in Iraqi Kurdistan, Yazidi kids play near a temple. Sheer joy. The ISIS genocide killed and enslaved thousands but didn't erase the community. https://t.co/GZxiodnTMZ

Amid the anniversary of the 2014 ISIS genocide this week, a small moment of joy. Yazidi children play in the wind and rain at a temple in the Iraqi Kurdistan region today. ISIS tried and failed to extinguish the community. https://t.co/Nn3WycDOn2

One of Iraq's most prominent human rights activists bids goodbye in Basra to her son who was kidnapped and shot dead. The government calls them unknown groups but everyone seems to know the groups carrying out these killings.

Peaceful student protest in Baghdad’s Tahrir square this evening calling for end to impunity for killers of activists. Iraqi military command issued orders barring photographers, video cameras. More security forces than protesters. https://t.co/uCufjUmRjk
My cat editor is not impressed… https://t.co/EtgkOnouUE

Drone attack on Baghdad airport Wednesday resulted in minor damage to State Department compound inside the airport complex, some treated for smoke inhalation. Today, U.S. offers up to $3 million reward for info on those targeting American diplomatic missions.

Mourning the tragic passing of Dr Nemam Ghafouri - founder of Joint Help for Kurdistan and an extraordinary human being who devoted the past five years of her life to helping #Yazidi women and children. She moved mountains. https://t.co/LdJiT07jyL

This may be the most pointed Twitter comment I've seen from a diplomat in #Iraq. The man below, who publicly accused an #Iran-backed militia of kidnapping his activist son two years ago, was shot in the head in the south of Iraq this week. Bravery is dangerous.
#Pope Francis arrives to rapturous welcome in Qaraqosh - biggest Christian town in #Iraq’s Ninevah plains and one celebrating its survival from ISIS. https://t.co/n6Bc5HQULc
#Pope Francis in Ur notes everyone has suffered from violence in #Iraq but specifically points out #Yazidis - the men killed, the women and children captured, those still missing.
A lovely piece.... Baghdad: forever on the brink https://t.co/yWvJuOksQk
Early spring weather in the beautiful #Kurdistan Region. https://t.co/fS1hyjcHQi

Nobel laureate Nadia Murad with the coffin of her brother - one of 103 #Yazidi victims of #ISIS genocide buried today in the #Iraqi village of Kocho after being identified by DNA testing. Likely thousands more in mass graves. https://t.co/9m0qArY4FI
In the #Yazidi village of Kocho, remains of 103 victims of #ISIS genocide returned to their families for burial today after being exhumed from a mass grave two years ago. https://t.co/wftLsLP1bH
#Baghdad! Not always what you think.

Striking that not a single person we talked to here believed it was #ISIS. They all believe #Iraqi political parties are jockeying for power and killing the poor in the process: Suicide Bombing in Crowded #Baghdad Market Kills at Least 32 https://t.co/v2DYuPifqY

Lovely to be in #Baghdad with ⁦@nytimes⁩. Where else can I have conversations like this? I asked Amar  Musa selling tinsel and Christmas trees in the street how business was and he recited one of his poems: ‘I am a cigarette and Iraq is the grave’. https://t.co/yQCLFBLV2z

A happier ending than most: #Mosul orphanage official tells #Yazidi mom in Canada today her daughter kidnapped six years ago by #ISIS has been identified. (video courtesy Sukaina Yunis) The girl's story here:  https://t.co/tv84bAZWIR https://t.co/EY3mBYBkE0

A happier ending to a terrible story. Sonya, the kidnapped daughter of a #Yazidi mom enslaved by #ISIS, walks out of a Mosul orphanage today after DNA tests confirm her identity. Her story here:  https://t.co/tv84bAZWIR (Photo courtesy Sukaina Yunis) https://t.co/OaEffEQiOY

My final @npr conversation with the wonderful @arishapiro @npract before joining @nytimes. Thank you to an enduringly great organization, cherished colleagues and editors and wonderful listeners. Photo Mosul 2017 w @SangarKhaleel  https://t.co/KFfSGqtkai https://t.co/ex2mUDHp1v

And the first visit by a pope to #Iraq. Saddam denied Vatican requests for a visit in fear of large gatherings he wasn't entirely in control of. Iraq is known as the land of prophets - including Ur - reputed birthplace of the Prophet Abraham.
Just a regular Thursday night traffic jam in #Baghdad... https://t.co/1IKivo3DFx
Thank you Diaa - I'm thrilled but sad to be leaving NPR - a truly wonderful place with amazing, talented and incredibly dedicated colleagues.

Hundreds of #Yazidi families moving home to #Sinjar after six years in displacement camps. Returning to damaged, looted houses and no electricity but as one returnee says ‘it’s better to have bread and water in your own home than meat and rice anywhere else’. https://t.co/T1gDjosoDH

#Jordan isn't really 'cautiously welcoming' the #UAE-Israel agreement with its polite statement, it's warning that the conflict will deepen unless this leads to an independent #Palestinian state - a prospect that seems further away with this deal.

Such sad news. #Iraq counter-terrorism expert and government advisor Hisham al-Hashimi shot dead tonight in Baghdad. There were few people who knew more about ISIS and were as generous with their knowledge.
Six years after #Yazidis lost their lives and their homes, why are children still living in tents in the mud? Anyone?
When this happens in the Middle East, people call them extremists...

UK court releases findings that #UAE's Sheikh Mohammed ordered the kidnapping of two of his daughters - one abducted after being sedated, the other subject to 'physical abuse amounting to torture'. Takes some of the shine off #Dubai. https://t.co/D1Yj2W6pvz
What did you do with the women? This looks like almost every official meeting in the Middle East.

I would like to cover an #Iraq where I don't have to write about 15-year-old boys sneaking out of the house to demonstrate for basic rights and being shot dead by security forces. Three of the latest protestors killed, including teenager Raymond Salem.

Wildly conflicting figures on how many but appeared to us to be to be at least a couple hundred thousand - some wire stories saying just 'thousands'. Extremely disciplined no matter the numbers. Sadrists and others sent their message and went home.

Twelve-year-old in Baghdad near the protest, left school after second grade. Sitting down because he's tired from  selling gum to support his brothers and sisters. Kids like him are part of the reason the protestors persist. #Iraqprotests https://t.co/riJB4axhmb
Air raid sirens coming from US embassy and explosion that sounded like rocket landing but unclear whether in #Baghdad green zone or nearby neighborhood.
Rocket attacks near Erbil apparently hit empty fields - inc unexploded rocket near airport. No attack on US consulate according to security officials.

Given the reports of bots behind this campaign, I think it's safe to say this is a real person, with a real message in Baghdad's real Tahrir square. A particularly brave one since so many young #Iraqis are being arrested or kidnapped for protesting.

Another #Baghdad rocket attack aiming at the US embassy that hit #Iraqi targets. Six civilians reported injured when rockets landed near entrance to green zone and in residential area not far from the embassy. Despite that #Iraqi forces continuing to protect the embassy area.
Explosion, presumably a rocket by the thud, in Baghdad green zone, followed by second explosion. Air raid sirens going off. No word yet on whether there were casualties.
@jeffersonmorley @ArianeTabatabai @realDonaldTrump The US-backed prime minister of Iraq was the source...Outgoing but still PM.

This is stunning - #Iraq prime minister tells parliament US troops should leave. Says @realDonaldTrump called him to ask him to mediate with #Iran and then ordered drone strike on Soleimani. Says Soleimani carrying response to Saudi initiative to defuse tension when he was hit.

After 2003 #Baghdad's airport road was one of the deadliest roads in #Iraq. It's been years since then - renovation included versions of iconic Iraqi paintings - now marked with shrapnel from the US drone strike. A lot of Iraqis fear being plunged back into the abyss. https://t.co/KwzRNUxp7o

One of bloodiest days in two months of #Iraq anti-government protests as security forces kill 27 people. Most of them in Nassriyah to keep protestors off a bridge. #Iran-backed militia now part of Iraqi security forces sends fighters to Najaf after #Iran embassy burned there.

Almost unbelievable - #Iranian consulate burned to the ground by #Iraqi protestors in Najaf. After setting it on fire, they blocked firefighters from putting it out. Diplomats believed evacuated weeks ago. This kind of anger won't be extinguished by wrangling over election law.

#Iraqi nurses in #Baghdad's Tahrir square. After their hospital shifts they come to treat the wounded near the front lines of the protest. 'My parents think I'm at work' one says. #IraqiProtests https://t.co/C8wjAEFeJz

Great point. We have lost the ability to report on US military deployments as they mostly engage with media through handouts these days. But things are so bad a lot of journalists and even editors don’t understand why running these photos without context is deeply wrong.

At #Baghdad protest today when I could open my eyes again after a cloud of tear gas descended, a policeman with a bunch of plastic roses with tiny white teddy bears handed me one. And I think that pretty much explains why #journalism is the best job in the world.

In #Baghdad where my hotel has received security orders banning #journalists from staying in rooms facing river or on high floors where you can see Tahrir Square protest unfolding. Transported to Saddam’s time when entire hotel wings were blocked so we couldn’t see things.

Statement from #Iraq military command saying US troops don't have approval to stay despite US assertions they will be deployed from #Syria to Western Iraq. US Administration seems to have neglected to consult the Iraq government... US defense secretary to discuss with Iraq today.

Iraqi women posing on steps of presidential palace after working with President @BarhamSalih on landmark draft bill to criminalize domestic violence. Controversial in #Iraq, the bill would end protection for assault within families and allow safe houses for victims of violence. https://t.co/7mHWeiAK38
He was wearing a home-made life jacket made of empty water bottles. Unimaginable the desperation that would lead to that.

In #Sinjar today. Beautiful #Yazidi holiday celebration at the Sharfadin temple. A day after commemoration of the #ISIS massacre in the Yazidi village of #Kocho in northern #Iraq. Smaller and more vulnerable but the community lives on. Stories coming @NPR https://t.co/UdARcjxwcg

Grateful to witness this. Cocho village in #Sinjar today on the fifth anniversary of the #ISIS massacre. The indescribable sorrow of #Yazidi women whose families were ripped apart in killings and  kidnappings at the heart of the #genocide. Stories coming @NPR https://t.co/YNVU7sCqxW
From Cocho village in Sinjar today - #Yazidi women mourning the more than 400 dead in the #ISIS massacre five years ago. #Yazidi #Genocide https://t.co/rVbcBuvl9U

French #ISIS member, Leonardo Mark, 33, Paris postal worker says ISIS declared him medically unfit for fighting. Says he taught in ISIS school and twice tried to escape. Asks forgiveness from Iraqis and to be sent back to #France. Baghdad court sentences him to death by hanging.

Absolutely extraordinary move in context of the region - #Yazidi religious leaders say Yazidi women who had children after being raped by #ISIS fighters can keep their children. Hundreds of women forced to give up them up to go home. Question now of whether they can find them.

Beautiful #Yazidi new year celebration on the mountain at Lalesh tonight. Five years after the #ISIS genocide, flames lit to celebrate earth reborn, victory of light over darkness. https://t.co/1qcs2SL3B8

On my way out of #Rojava #Syria with two particularly lasting memories - told by ISIS wives at Camp Hol I'm an infidel who needs to cover my hair and young village men on a flooded road cheerfully standing in the water to show us where the road ends and get us safely across.

There's a boy lying here in #Mosul's old city last year. Not clear if he was from an Arab #ISIS family or a Yazidi captive but he was a boy, killed in the fighting. He's not one of the 800+ children recorded killed. He was buried in a mass grave. We should not forget him. https://t.co/VamOLG9luJ
Nice to hear #Kurdish flight announcement on #Iraqi Airways in addition to Arabic and English. Might seem like a small thing but hugely important.

An #Iraqi treasure is gone. Dr Lamia al-Gailani. An #archaeologist in #Iraq and London for the past half-century currently working on the new #Basra museum, passed away suddenly in Amman. Dr Gailani had an infectious love of archaeology and of life. She will be greatly missed. https://t.co/I8D9598VlV

A moment I didn’t think I’d see - #Iraqi musician serenades US Marine General Austin Renforth and Iraqi General Jalil Rubaie on very relaxed walk-about among the crowds in downtown #Baghdad https://t.co/N5tLjTIovJ
Amazing. A concert near the building in #Mosul where ISIS threw people to their deaths.

#Mosul night-life. A well-kept secret - after ISIS east Mosul comes alive at night. Restaurants on the river packed with people. Amusement parks and boat rides on the Tigris. Families who can't afford any of those picnic on the sidewalk. https://t.co/xZn5LlgWoC
In #Basra's Aleea neighborhood. As we walked through one of the poorest neighborhoods in the poorest big city in #Iraq, people kept inviting us for lunch. https://t.co/Ybu5PMIJyL

Staggering number of people sentenced today in #Egypt's Rabaa square trial. 75 given death sentences, 47 life in prison, and hundreds more, inc American Moustafa Kassem, to 15 years. Five people died in prison awaiting trial for the 2013 protest against Egypt's military coup.

#Mosul Book Festival - crowds lining up for donated books. #ISIS banned non-religious books, music and almost everything else. Now there's a hunger for culture again. Thousands of people came out for books, music and theatre in a park where ISIS trained child fighters. https://t.co/e61DRevO74

The girls of #Mosul salute you. Handing out sweets and greeting visitors at today’s Book Festival under the title ‘I am Iraqi - I read’. Thousands of people enjoying books and music at a park where #ISIS trained child fighters. https://t.co/mCE5RVUO7x
Voting in #Baghdad - 84 year old voter and his wife, 75, in Athamiya neighborhood. They say they’re hopeful for the future.  #Iraq #elections https://t.co/aM9tGI4yUT

In #Iraq's post-ISIS #Mosul - a not exactly licensed but very cheerful new fast-food place. 'Iraq is a country of wonders - you can find anything here' says the owner when I express surprise to see the golden arches in Mosul. @npr #RoadTrip https://t.co/U5vfNmRzc1
#Kurdish leader Massoud Barzani tells @NPR in first interview since referendum no regrets on vote despite consequences. https://t.co/bqx4OOlEN2
First day of school in #Mosul - with #ISIS gone, public schools open for first time in three years. Few books though. https://t.co/odGl3lSqqz
The only non-Kurdish flag at rally going on now in #Erbil. Israel the sole country to support independence referendum https://t.co/MMGxTsOrLN
The only non-Kurdish flag at rally going on now in #Erbil. Israel the sole country to support independence referendum https://t.co/kGf2jKxhfi
For Malala, Now 20, Birthdays Are Best Spent With Girls Who Dream Big - my @NPR story on inspiring young women https://t.co/hfOgxD20mN
#Iraqi man escaping # Mosul with his nightingale. Keeping birds banned under #ISIS. He says he hid it for three years. https://t.co/33afgOo4sF
Iraqi Yazidis - among the biggest ISIS victims - denied boarding on US-bound flights despite visas. Cleared Iraqi refugees also barred.
#Kurdish president @masoud_barzani talks Mosul, #Iran and prisoners held by IS in my PBS @NewsHourWorld interview http://t.co/bPk6q1HRkB
